finding_results.notes: The breakfast aftermath now states the table pattern once, removes the repeated family/outsider paraphrase, and adds a concrete social beat where Ren sets the last flat cake on Jet's plate and gets pulled into the meal's teasing rhythm.
finding_results.summary: Closing breakfast movement compressed; repetition cut; Ren shifts from observer to participant through a visible table action while her outsider recognition is reduced to a single closing sentence.

finding_results.notes: All five cited admissions reworked to add on-page pressure markers. Spike's cargo-code speech (line 143) now has a false start ('Some delay notice I never—') breaking the clean three-beat list, and the closer trails into 'Everyone wants you to have known what it meant' instead of the quotable 'cleaner version after the fact.' Spike's banality-of-evil speech (line 171) now trails off mid-thought ('Nobody thinks they're—') and the four-beat thematic ladder is broken into uneven concrete fragments. Ren's wrong/alive admission (line 329) now has a restart ('and it was still—'), grounds the knowing in age ('I was old enough to know'), and the follow-up exchange shifts from 'tidy' pushback to 'Old enough to know and nobody stopped' / 'Nobody could afford to stop.' Ren's persona-stripping speech (line 355) compresses the escalating five-step list into less staged action-based description with a qualification ('That was the hard part, I think'). Ren's 'She loved me' (line 381) arrives without the dramatic-timing buildup and the triple-negative narrator evaluation is replaced by Ren's own qualification ('I don't know if that helps. But it's what I've got').

Both narrator-evaluates-dialogue instances removed. At line 101, 'That landed harder than if he'd said yes' replaced with Faye's physical reaction only ('sat up the rest of the way, slowly, then stopped with one hand flat on the cot'); the impact registers through her body and her subsequent clipped 'Right' / 'Yeah' exchange. At line 225, the entire evaluative sentence ('That was dry enough to count as a joke. It landed and neither of them reached for it.') removed; the paragraph break between 'Dogs are less predictable' and 'Faye looked at the laid-out parts' now carries the beat silently.

finding_results.notes: Reworked the chapter's recurring negation/correction beats across the Jet, Faye/Ren, and breakfast movements into mixed syntactic strategies, including image-led emphasis, embedded contrast, action-led emphasis, and plain declarative turns. Preserved scene pressure, character register, and continuity while reducing the repeated fallback cadence.
finding_results.summary: The repeated 'Not X. Y.' / 'It wasn't X. It was Y.' stress pattern has been broken across the chapter's key emphasis points without flattening the prose.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised the breakfast ending to resolve the craft repetition: one interpretive outsider beat remains, the rest is carried by table behavior and a new Ren-triggered shift in the group's dynamic.

Revised seven passages across two findings. Finding 001: roughened five polished admissions by adding false starts, trailing-off, qualifications, physical stress-tells, and concrete detours — Spike no longer delivers clean four-beat thematic ladders and Ren no longer resolves conflict in perfectly balanced declaratives. Finding 002: removed two instances of narrator commentary evaluating how dialogue landed, replacing with physical reaction beats that let the reader infer impact from behavior. All edits preserve original content and emotional weight while adding friction consistent with each character's style-bible voice profile. Heading contract preserved. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Contraction levels verified consistent with style bible settings.
Revised chapter_17 for the prose/copyedit pass, resolving the packeted cadence issue while preserving heading, continuity, and character voice.
